_meta._: Full English locale (parity with fr.json). Updates to FR keys still fall through automatically via the deep-merge in src/i18n/request.ts.

about.metaDescription: Digizelle, a French non-profit (loi 1901) for digital inclusion founded in 2023 in Paris. More than 20 members with diverse talents (design, web dev, communication, marketing).
about.title: Digital inclusion,
about.body: Digizelle's mission is to inspire, educate and support young people — especially young women — on their journey into the digital world. Founded in 2023 in Paris, the association now brings together more than 20 members from diverse backgrounds: design, development, communication, marketing, events. Our goal: to create a stimulating and supportive environment where everyone can grow their potential through concrete, high-impact projects.

0.title: Inclusion
0.desc: Digital for everyone, regardless of skill level, background or gender.

1.title: Innovation
1.desc: New formats, rooted in the technologies of today and tomorrow.

2.title: Community
2.desc: Learn together. Move forward together. Succeed together.

3.title: Kindness

0.title: Digizelle is founded
0.desc: Priscillia Meza Samira and Bleuenn Cloteaux found the association in Paris around four pillars: events, communication, development, design.

1.title: 20+ active members
1.desc: The team grows with students from Epitech, EPITA, IONIS and beyond. First masterclasses and workshops.

2.title: Women in GenAI Hackathon
2.desc: Digizelle team reaches the finals — co-organized by EY and Microsoft. A showcase for women's talent in tech.

3.title: VivaTech 2025
3.desc: Digizelle present at the Microsoft booth. "AI, my future career" workshop hosted by Priscillia Meza Samira for ages 15-21.

4.title: BECOMTECH × Epitech
4.desc: "Women in Tech: breaking imposter syndrome" workshop — Epitech Le Kremlin-Bicêtre, June 2025.

5.title: Digizelle Impact Event 2026
5.desc: March 13, 2026 at Epitech Paris. Theme: "Leadership & Innovation — building the future together." Keynote, panels, strategic networking.

mission.title: Build an inclusive space where everyone grows at their own pace.
mission.body: Whatever your level, background or means — every person deserves access to digital, to its tools, to its opportunities.

vision.title: A digital generation rising through community.
vision.body: Young people don't just consume tech, they create it. With them, we build models of knowledge-sharing that set a new standard.

blog.metaDescription: The Digizelle journal: opinion pieces, advice, lessons learned, tech & inclusion news.
blog.title: Reads, ideas,

featured.label: Featured · {category}
featured.title: Why mentorship changes everything for young developers
featured.author: Priscillia Meza
featured.body: Three years after the first Mentora matches, we step back to look at what works, what doesn't, and what we'd love to see across the French tech scene.

0.title: How to choose your digital training in 2026
0.author: Sofia K.
0.excerpt: Bootcamp, university, MOOC or self-taught? Seven criteria to make the right choice without regret.
0.body:
The landscape of digital training has grown so dense that it's often hard to see clearly. This article gives you seven concrete criteria — career outcomes, support, funding, teaching approach, community, stance on AI and personal fit — to compare your options.

Our belief at Digizelle: the best training is the one that matches you, not the one that sells the best. Take time to test, talk to alumni, and visit before signing up.

1.title: Looking back at Hackathon #3 — what we learned
1.author: Marwane O.
1.excerpt: 18 teams, 48 hours, a demanding jury — an honest debrief on what worked, and what we'd change.
1.body:
Our third hackathon just wrapped and we learned a lot. On the positive side: diversity of profiles paid off — mixed teams produced the most accomplished projects. On the to-improve side: the Sunday night timing was too tight.

The winning team "TerreClaire" delivered a carbon footprint analysis tool for local nonprofits. Reproducible, open-source, elegant. We've invited them to pitch in May with our partners.

2.title: Generative AI: 5 tools to know for your projects
2.author: Hugo P.
2.excerpt: A short, opinionated selection of generative AI tools that genuinely change our day-to-day as a product team.
2.body:
We spent six months integrating generative AI into our workflows. Here are the five tools we're keeping: Claude for long-form writing and code, Cursor for the augmented editor, Notion AI for synthesized note-taking, Midjourney for brand illustrations, and Whisper for automatic session transcription.

The real shift isn't the tool but the mindset: learning to articulate what you want, iterate fast, and correct without frustration. That's what we teach in our 2026 masterclasses.

3.title: Diversity in tech: where do we stand in 2026?
3.author: Inès L.
3.excerpt: Three years after the big announcements of 2023, where does diversity in French tech really stand? Data, testimonies, paths forward.
3.body:
The official numbers are mixed. While the share of women in tech schools has risen from 18 to 24%, the glass ceiling remains thick as you climb the hierarchy. And some minorities are still invisible in the statistics.

At Digizelle we're working on three fronts: making role models visible through Mentora, opening workshops to non-traditional audiences, and funding targeted scholarships. Diversity isn't decreed, it's built.

4.title: Becoming a mentor: a practical guide
4.author: Marwane O.
4.excerpt: Fifteen minutes a month is enough. How to start out as a Digizelle mentor, and what we really expect from you.
4.body:
Becoming a mentor doesn't require being a senior expert. Three years of experience is enough, and most importantly: the desire to share what you know. Our matching offers you a mentee who fits your availability, skills and energy.

A session lasts 30 minutes a month. We give you a starting framework, resources to go further, and a referent to chat with other mentors. The rest comes naturally.

5.title: Our 2026 stack — how we work
5.author: Hugo P.
5.excerpt: Next.js 16, Supabase, Resend, Vercel — a status update on the technical choices that hold the house together.
5.body:
Our stack evolved in 2026. We moved to Next.js 16 right at release to take advantage of stabilized Server Actions and the React 19 transition API. Supabase remains our single source for data and auth, hosted in Europe.

On the DX side, we adopted Cursor for the editor, Linear for the roadmap, and Resend for transactional email. The Vercel + Supabase + Resend integration easily saves us 20 hours a month on operations. More time for the product.

0.q: How long until I get a reply?
0.a: We get back to you within 48 working hours. For press inquiries, expect a response within 24 hours.

1.q: Can I become a mentor without being a senior expert?
1.a: Yes — we welcome all experience levels from 3 years on the job. Teaching ability matters as much as your tech background.

2.a: Most of our programs are free or fairly priced for participants. Sponsorships fund the bulk of it.

3.q: How do I propose a partnership?
3.a: Select "Partnership" in the form and describe your organization. We'll set up a video call within 5 days.

cookies.metaDescription: Digizelle cookie policy — types of cookies used, purposes, duration, how to manage them.
cookies.title: Cookies &
cookies.intro: The digizelle.fr site uses a minimal number of cookies. This page explains what they do, how long they live, and how to control them. No advertising or third-party profiling cookies are placed. This policy is written in accordance with article 82 of the French Data Protection Act and the 2020-2021 guidelines from the CNIL (French data protection authority).

essential.title: Essential cookies
essential.body: Required for the site to function: login session (next-auth.session-token), anti-CSRF token (next-auth.csrf-token), theme preference (digizelle-theme). They do not require prior consent under article 82 of the French Data Protection Act.
essential.duration: Duration: browsing session or up to 12 months for preferences

analytics.title: Audience measurement cookies
analytics.body: Aggregated and anonymized statistics to understand site usage and improve the experience. Configured according to the CNIL exemption (truncated IP, limited lifetime, no cross-referencing with other processing). No identifiable personal data is collected.

preferences.title: Preferences (cookie + local storage)
preferences.body: Remember your interface choices: language (NEXT_LOCALE cookie) and dark/light theme (digizelle-theme localStorage). They improve browsing comfort. The NEXT_LOCALE cookie is essential for displaying your chosen language; the theme is purely optional.
preferences.duration: Duration: 12 months for language, permanent for theme (can be cleared at any time)

list.title: Detailed inventory
list.intro: Complete list of items stored on your device — HTTP cookies and browser local storage — placed or potentially placed. The names below are the exact technical identifiers visible in your browser's developer tools (DevTools → Application). This list is updated whenever the site evolves technically.
list.tableLabel: Inventory of cookies and local storage
list.cookiesSubtitle: Sent with every request to the server. Visible in DevTools → Application → Cookies.
list.storageSubtitle: Kept only in your browser, never sent to the server. Visible in DevTools → Application → Local Storage.

0.name: next-auth.session-token
0.purpose: Maintains your signed-in session in the Mentora member area

1.name: next-auth.csrf-token
1.purpose: Protection against cross-site request forgery attacks

0.name: digizelle-theme
0.purpose: Stores your light / dark display preference

1.name: digizelle-cookie-consent
1.purpose: Stores your choice on the cookie banner (accepted / refused / categories)

thirdParty.title: Third-party cookies
thirdParty.body:
As of the last update, the digizelle.fr site does not place any third-party cookies for advertising, profiling or sharing with external platforms (Meta, Google Ads, TikTok, etc.).

Third-party videos or content (YouTube, Vimeo) that may be embedded in the future will be loaded via "privacy-enhanced" modes or a double-click mechanism, so external resources are only loaded with your explicit consent.

consent.title: Consent & withdrawal
consent.body:
On your first visit, a banner offers you the choice to accept, refuse or customize non-essential cookies. Refusing is just as easy as accepting — a single click is enough.

• If you accept: the chosen categories are activated and your preference is stored for 6 months.
• If you refuse: only essential cookies are placed.
• If you customize: you activate categories one by one.

At any time, you can reopen the panel via the "Manage my preferences" link in the footer. Your choice is applied immediately and can be revoked. The banner is automatically re-shown every 6 months to collect fresh consent, in line with CNIL guidelines.

manage.title: Manage cookies in your browser
manage.body:
In addition to the banner, you can clear or block cookies at any time via your browser settings. Note: blocking essential cookies will prevent you from signing in to your member area.

Useful links:
• Chrome: Settings → Privacy and security → Cookies
• Firefox: Settings → Privacy & Security → Cookies and Site Data
• Safari: Settings → Privacy → Block cookies
• Edge: Settings → Cookies and site permissions

The CNIL also provides an educational tool: www.cnil.fr/fr/cookies-les-outils-pour-les-maitriser.

contact.title: Have a question?
contact.body: For any question about our use of cookies, write to us at contact@digizelle.fr (subject: "Cookies"). You can also consult our privacy policy for details on the related data processing.

banner.title: Cookies
banner.body: We only use cookies that are essential for the site to work, plus optional preference and audience measurement cookies. You choose what you accept.

preferences.title: Cookie preferences
preferences.subtitle: Enable or disable categories based on your choices. You can come back at any time.
preferences.essentialDesc: Required for the site to function (session, security). Always active.
preferences.preferencesDesc: Remember your interface choices like light/dark theme or language.
preferences.analyticsDesc: Anonymized statistics to understand site usage and improve it.

faqPage.metaDescription: All the answers about Digizelle, sign-up, programs, Mentora mentorship, the community and the protection of your data.
faqPage.title: Frequently asked
faqPage.intro: A question before you get started? Here are the answers we give most often. If yours isn't here, write to us at contact@digizelle.fr — we reply within 48 working hours.

search.label: Search the FAQ
search.placeholder: Type a keyword: mentor, sign-up, free…
search.noResults: No question matches "{query}". Try another keyword, or write to us at contact@digizelle.fr.
search.matchCount: {count, plural, =0 {No results} one {1 question found} other {# questions found}}

faqPage.ctaBody: Write to us, we read every message. Personal reply within 48 working hours.

what.a: Digizelle is a French non-profit (association loi 1901), founded in 2023, based at EPITECH Le Kremlin-Bicêtre. Our mission: include and grow young people in the digital field — through mentorship, workshops, hackathons and a supportive community. We are 100% non-profit.

who.a: For anyone curious about digital, from age 15. We particularly welcome young people who haven't had the chance to be exposed to tech (social or geographic background, atypical paths, underrepresentation). No barriers of skill level, education or status.

free.a: Yes for participants: all our programs (workshops, masterclasses, hackathons, Mentora) are free or fairly priced. We are funded by corporate sponsorships (Microsoft, EY, AWS, Allianz, Epitech, HEC Paris, Station F, BECOMTECH…) and by public grants.

associationStatus.a: Yes — a French non-profit (association loi 1901), declared at the prefecture in 2023. Registered office at EPITECH Le Kremlin-Bicêtre Campus, 14-16 rue Voltaire, 94270 Le Kremlin-Bicêtre. All legal information is on the /legal page.

team.a: A team of volunteers: a board (presidency, treasury, secretariat), Team Leaders for Communication, Mentorship, Tech and Events, and a growing community of mentors and volunteers. Everyone contributes their time on top of their studies or job.

how.a: Click "Sign in" at the top right, then "Create an account." Once your email is confirmed, you'll have access to your member area where you can join the community or request a mentor.

data.a: The minimum: first name, email, password. Optional: last name, photo, bio, skills. For minors (15-17 years old), we also ask for the year of birth to apply the reinforced protection required by GDPR.

age.q: What's the minimum age to sign up?
age.a: From age 15, in accordance with article 8 of the GDPR (digital age of consent in France). For 15-17 year olds, no advertising profiling is applied and moderation is reinforced. If you're under 15, your parents can contact contact@digizelle.fr for supervised access.

delete.a: Yes, at any time, from Community → Settings → Danger zone. You then have 30 days to change your mind before the irreversible purge. This deletion covers all your data: account, profile, posts, messages, hosted media.

forgotPassword.a: On the sign-in page, click "Forgot your password?" — you'll receive a reset link by email (valid for 1 hour). If the email doesn't arrive, check your spam folder or write to contact@digizelle.fr.

twoFactor.a: Recommended for everyone, mandatory for administrators, moderators and active mentors. Enable it in /account/2fa with any authenticator app (Google Authenticator, 1Password, Authy…). Remember to save your 10 backup codes.

list.a:
Four complementary formats:
• Immersive Workshop — 3-day hands-on sessions to discover a skill
• Masterclass — intensive course with an expert to deepen your knowledge
• Hackathon — 48 hours in a team to build on a challenge with mentors
• Mentora — long-term 1-to-1 mentorship program

level.a: No prerequisites. Our formats range from complete beginner (first discovery workshop) to advanced (tech masterclass). We adapt to your background, not the other way around. Motivation and curiosity matter more than technical mastery.

prepare.q: How do I prepare for a workshop or hackathon?
prepare.a: Nothing to prepare in advance — all teaching materials are provided. Bring a laptop if you have one (otherwise we can lend you one on site). A curious attitude and the desire to learn are enough.

certificate.a: For masterclasses and hackathons, yes — a Digizelle certificate of participation is given to attendees present for the entire session. It's not a state-recognized diploma, but it's official proof useful for your CV.

where.q: Where do the events take place?
where.a: Mainly in Paris and Île-de-France (EPITECH, HEC Paris, Station F, partner venues). Some formats are also 100% online. Details for each event are published on /events at least 3 weeks in advance.

what.a: Mentora is our 1-to-1 mentorship program: we connect you with an experienced mentor in the digital field that interests you (dev, design, data, product, AI…). You meet over video at a pace you choose together — typically one hour every two weeks over 3 to 6 months.

findMentor.q: How do I find a mentor?
findMentor.a: Once registered with Mentora, you fill out your mentee profile (goals, skills to develop, languages, time zone) and our algorithm suggests compatible mentors. You can also browse the open catalog at /mentora/discover and send a request to anyone you're interested in.

becomeMentor.q: How do I become a mentor?
becomeMentor.a: Create your mentor profile at /mentora/become-mentor. You present your background, your skills, your availability. The team reviews your profile within 5 working days. It's open to any professional with 2+ years of experience who wants to give their time.

paid.a: No, mentorship is entirely volunteer-based. Mentors give their time freely. For mentees, it's also free. That's our ethical commitment: helping young people shouldn't depend on their means.

duration.q: How long does a mentorship last?
duration.a: You decide with your mentor — typically 3 to 6 months, with one to two hours per month. You can end the relationship at any time or request a mentor change if the matching isn't working.

matching.a: The algorithm takes into account: skills sought vs offered, languages, time zone, mentor's average rating, response time, current load, and a diversity factor to avoid bias. You can see the score breakdown for each suggested mentor via the "Why this match?" button.

what.a: It's our online space where members, mentors and alumni discuss, share resources, ask questions, organize challenges. You access it after sign-up, from the /community menu. It's also where we announce events and where moderation looks out for everyone.

rules.a: Mutual respect first and foremost. No discrimination, no harassment, no spoilers of ongoing challenges. Confidentiality of mentorship sessions. The full charter is on /community/charte — reading it is mandatory at onboarding.

moderation.a: A team of moderators, identifiable by a badge, reviews reports. Graduated sanctions: warning → mute → suspension → ban. Bans require two distinct moderators (proposal + confirmation) to avoid arbitrary decisions.

report.a: On every post or comment, the "Report" button opens a menu. Choose the category (spam, harassment, illegal content…) and add context if you'd like. Reporting is anonymous — neither the author nor other members know who reported.

appeal.q: I was wrongly sanctioned, what can I do?
appeal.a: You can appeal by email to contact@digizelle.fr. Reply within 14 days. The reason for the sanction and the author of the decision are in your moderation history on the admin side (visible to you via the GDPR data export).

protection.a: TLS 1.3 across the whole site, hashed passwords (bcrypt cost ≥ 12), Row-Level Security on the PostgreSQL database, hosting in the European Union (Vercel CDG, Supabase eu-west-3 Paris), 2FA mandatory for privileged accounts. We never sell any data and do no advertising profiling.

export.a: Yes, in accordance with article 20 of the GDPR (right to portability). From Community → Settings → My data, you can export your data in JSON format, machine-readable. The export contains your profile, your posts, your messages, your moderation history.

newsletter.q: How do I unsubscribe from the newsletter?
newsletter.a: "Unsubscribe" link at the bottom of every email — a single click is enough. You can also do it from Community → Settings → Email preferences.

rights.q: How do I exercise my GDPR rights?
rights.a: Most rights are accessible directly from your member area: access (JSON export), rectification (profile editing), erasure (account deletion). For any right not covered by the built-in tools (restriction, objection, post-mortem), write to contact@digizelle.fr — reply within 30 days.

minor.q: I'm a minor, can my parents act on my behalf?
minor.a: Yes — your parents or guardians can at any time request access, rectification or deletion of your data via contact@digizelle.fr, providing proof of their status (family record book, court ruling). For those under 15, sign-up itself requires their authorization.

violation.a: We notify the CNIL within 72 hours (article 33 GDPR) and individually inform the people concerned if the breach presents a high risk to their rights. More details in our internal GDPR runbook.

footer.tagline: An inclusive digital community where diversity, innovation and learning meet.
footer.heading: Ready to join?

home.metaDescription: Digizelle is a French non-profit (loi 1901) founded in 2023, dedicated to including and empowering young people in the digital field.
home.heroBody: Our mission: to inspire, educate and support young people on their digital journey. More than a non-profit — a community where diversity, innovation and digital learning meet.

manifesto.body: Digizelle works every day so young people — especially young women — across the Paris region and beyond find their place in tech. Workshops, masterclasses, hackathons, the Mentora program and an online community: a portfolio of formats to move from “I would love to” to “I am doing it”.

inclusion.title: Everyone, for real.
inclusion.body: We do not fake it: our cohorts are mixed, multi-background, multi-age. All our programs are free or fairly priced for young learners.

mentorship.title: A human in front of you, not a chatbot.
mentorship.body: Mentora pairs every mentee with a volunteer mentor for 6 months. Algorithmic matching, video sessions, shared calendar, clear goals and unlimited messaging.

community.title: We learn better together.
community.body: Our online community (200+ active members) gathers themed lounges, badges, regular challenges and daily peer support. Kind by default.

atelier.title: Immersive Workshop
atelier.desc: Hands-on 3-day sessions to try out a digital skill.

masterclass.title: Masterclass
masterclass.desc: Intensive classes with experts to dig into a technical area.

hackathon.title: Hackathon
hackathon.desc: 48 hours to build in teams on a digital challenge with mentors.

mentoraCta.title: Mentora — your digital mentor,
mentoraCta.body: A mentorship program combining humans and AI. Find your mentor, meet on video, track your progress and unlock resources tailored to your journey.

items.title: Digizelle Impact 2026 — Epitech Paris
items.intro: First edition of Digizelle Impact, our annual gathering of youth, mentors, partner companies and decision-makers around digital inclusion.

items.title: Women in Tech roundtable — HEC Paris
items.intro: Inspiring roundtable with women leaders in tech, hosted as part of the Women in Tech cycle at HEC Paris.

items.title: Women in GenAI Hackathon — EY × Microsoft
items.intro: 48 hours of building around generative AI, in teams with mentors and expert coaches from EY and Microsoft.

items.title: VivaTech 2025 — “AI my future career” workshop
items.intro: Discovery workshop “AI my future career” at Europe’s biggest tech show — demos, exchanges and career guidance.

items.title: BECOMTECH × Epitech — Imposter syndrome
items.intro: Talk and discussion on imposter syndrome in tech career paths, in partnership with BECOMTECH and Epitech.

items.name: Lina, 19
items.quote: Before Digizelle I thought tech was not for me. Three days later I had coded my first interface. The mentor who supported me became a real friend.

items.name: Idriss, 22
items.quote: I came alone and left with a team, a project, a prize and three job interviews. Digizelle does not push you — they make you capable.

items.name: Salma, 24
items.quote: My mentor works at Doctolib. We meet for an hour a month and it has become my best career tool. The format is exactly right.

communityCta.title: Passionate about digital and want to be part of a vibrant, inclusive community?
communityCta.body: Become a Digizelle member and take part in our events, training and innovative projects. Together, let us inspire and support young talents on their digital journey.

items.a: For any young person (and not-so-young) curious about digital, whatever your level, background or path. Motivation is enough.
All our programs are free or fairly priced for participants. Corporate sponsorship and partnerships fund most of it.
No prerequisites. Whether you are a beginner or already in tech, we have a format for you. Curiosity and commitment are enough.
Create your account or write to us via the contact form. We get back to you within 48 hours to direct you to the right program.

legal.metaDescription: Legal notice for the Digizelle association — publisher, hosting, intellectual property, liability.
legal.title: Legal
legal.intro: This site is published by Digizelle, a French non-profit (association loi 1901), with its registered office at EPITECH Le Kremlin-Bicêtre Campus, 14-16 rue Voltaire, 94270 Le Kremlin-Bicêtre. This legal notice governs the use of digizelle.fr and its subdomains, in accordance with articles 6-III and 19 of French law no. 2004-575 of June 21, 2004 on confidence in the digital economy (LCEN).

publisher.title: Site publisher
publisher.body:
Digizelle — French non-profit (association loi 1901), declared at the prefecture in 2023.
Registered office: EPITECH Le Kremlin-Bicêtre Campus, 14-16 rue Voltaire, 94270 Le Kremlin-Bicêtre, France
Single contact email: contact@digizelle.fr

The association's purpose is the inclusion and growth of young people in the digital field: workshops, masterclasses, hackathons, the Mentora mentorship program and public events. Editorial responsibility lies with the association's board.

registration.title: Registration & RNA
registration.body:
Digizelle is an association governed by the French law of July 1, 1901 and the decree of August 16, 1901, declared at the Paris prefecture.
• RNA number: W751XXXXXX (to be replaced with the official number)
• Declaration date: 2023
• Purpose declared in the Official Journal of associations: to promote the inclusion and growth of young people in the digital field

The association is non-profit and is not registered with the trade and companies register (RCS). It does not have an intra-EU VAT number.

host.title: Hosting
host.body:
The site is hosted on the Vercel Inc. platform, 340 S Lemon Ave #4133, Walnut, CA 91789, United States (execution region: Europe — CDG / Paris). Site: https://vercel.com — Contact: https://vercel.com/contact

The PostgreSQL database, authentication and file storage are operated by Supabase, Inc., 970 Toa Payoh North #07-04, Singapore 318992 (application region: eu-west-3 / Paris). Site: https://supabase.com

Transactional emails and newsletter campaigns are sent via Resend, Inc., 2261 Market Street #5039, San Francisco, CA 94114, United States (European Union routing).

Observability and error reporting are provided by Functional Software Inc. (Sentry), 132 Hawthorne Street, San Francisco, CA 94107, United States (EU region: sentry.de.io).

director.title: Editorial direction
director.body: Editorial direction is handled by the Digizelle association's board. Content published on this site is reviewed and approved collegially before being put online. Any request for correction, right of reply or relating to content can be sent to contact@digizelle.fr; we commit to responding within 30 days.

ip.title: Intellectual property
ip.body:
The entire site (text, illustrations, logo, mascots, visual identity, photographs, source code) is the exclusive property of Digizelle or its partners, and is protected by the French intellectual property code (articles L.111-1 and following).

Any reproduction, representation, modification, publication, adaptation or distribution, in whole or in part, by any means whatsoever, is prohibited without prior written authorization, except for strict press use, subject to compliance with the provisions of article L.122-5 of the intellectual property code.

The trademarks, logos and trade names of partners mentioned remain the property of their respective holders.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.body:
Digizelle strives to maintain the accuracy of the information published on the site, without being able to guarantee its completeness, ongoing accuracy or absence of errors. The association declines any responsibility for the use that may be made of this information and the direct or indirect consequences that may result.

Hyperlinks pointing to third-party sites do not engage Digizelle's responsibility for the content of those sites. Digizelle cannot be held responsible for any damages related to the use of the site (service interruption, data loss, computer viruses) despite the technical measures put in place.

cgu.title: General terms of use
cgu.body:
Access to and use of the digizelle.fr site implies full and complete acceptance of these terms:
• Use of the site in compliance with applicable laws and public order
• Prohibition of any intrusion attempt, mass scraping or intentional server overload
• Account creation reserved for people over 15 years old (parental consent required below)
• Respect for other members in community spaces (no discriminatory remarks, harassment or illegal content)

Detailed general terms of use apply to the Mentora member area; they are communicated upon registration.

credits.title: Credits & technologies
credits.body:
Design and development: Digizelle's Design & Dev team, with the support of community volunteers.

Technical stack:
• Next.js 16 (React framework, App Router)
• TypeScript for code reliability
• Supabase for the PostgreSQL database and authentication
• Auth.js (NextAuth) for session management
• Vercel for hosting and CDN
• Resend for transactional emails

Original icons and illustrations created by the Digizelle Design team. Font: Signika (Anna Giedryś, SIL Open Font License).

contact.title: Contact
contact.body:
For any question relating to this legal notice, use of the site, a GDPR request or to exercise a right of reply: contact@digizelle.fr (the association's single contact address).

Postal mail: Digizelle, EPITECH Le Kremlin-Bicêtre Campus, 14-16 rue Voltaire, 94270 Le Kremlin-Bicêtre, France.

updates.title: Updates to this notice
updates.body:
This legal notice may be modified at any time, without prior notice, to reflect changes to the site, partnerships or regulations. The date of the last update is shown at the top of the page. We invite you to consult this page regularly.

In the event of a substantial change (e.g. change of host or editorial director), additional information may be temporarily displayed on the home page.

mentora.metaDescription: Digizelle's 1-to-1 mentorship platform connecting young people with digital professionals.

mentora.body: Mentora is our 1-to-1 mentorship platform that connects Digizelle young people with digital professionals. Smart matching, video calls, progress tracking and personalized resources.

how.intro: Three steps to start your journey with a mentor.

0.desc: A smart questionnaire identifies your ambitions, your availability and your current level.

howItWorks.title: Four steps
howItWorks.intro: From account creation to your first mentor video call, expect less than a week.

0.title: Create your profile

1.title: Explore mentors

3.desc: Monthly video calls, private messaging, progress tracking and tailored resources.

privacy.metaDescription: Digizelle privacy policy — data collected, purposes, retention, GDPR rights.
privacy.title: Your data,
privacy.intro: Digizelle takes the protection of your personal data very seriously. This policy describes, with full transparency, what data we collect, why, how it is protected, how long it is kept, and how you can exercise your rights in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR) and the French Data Protection Act of January 6, 1978 as amended.

controller.title: Data controller
controller.body:
Digizelle (French non-profit, association loi 1901) acts as the data controller within the meaning of article 4.7 of the GDPR for all personal data collected via this site and the Mentora & Community platform.

Contact details:
• Registered office: EPITECH Le Kremlin-Bicêtre Campus, 14-16 rue Voltaire, 94270 Le Kremlin-Bicêtre, France
• Single association contact: contact@digizelle.fr (all requests: general, GDPR, communication, press)

The role of GDPR contact point is held by the association's Tech Lead until an external delegate is appointed. For any request to exercise rights, report a breach, or discuss the compliance of processing, write to contact@digizelle.fr. The contact details for the CNIL (the French supervisory authority) are listed at the end of this document.

data.title: Data collected
data.body:
We apply the principle of minimization: only data strictly necessary for each purpose is collected.

— Contact form: first name, last name, email, subject, message. Legal basis: legitimate interest (responding to your request).
— Mentora sign-up: first name, last name, email, hashed password (bcrypt), role (learner / mentor / partner), sign-up date. Legal basis: performance of the membership contract.
— Mentor/mentee profile: skills, availability, background, optional photo. Legal basis: explicit consent.
— Newsletter: email, sign-up source, date. Legal basis: consent (opt-in).
— Technical data: truncated IP address, user-agent, pages visited (server logs, aggregated statistics).
— Technical cookies: session, CSRF token, theme preference. See the Cookies page.

No sensitive data (origin, political opinions, religion, health, sexual orientation) is collected.

purpose.title: Purposes
purpose.body:
Your data is used exclusively for the following purposes:
• Responding to your requests (contact, press, partnership)
• Managing your account in the Mentora area and the mentor/mentee matching
• Sending you the Digizelle newsletter, which you've freely subscribed to
• Informing you of events or programs you've registered for
• Ensuring the security, availability and improvement of the site
• Responding to legal obligations (judicial requisitions)

Your data is never sold. No advertising profiling is carried out.

retention.title: Retention period
retention.body:
In accordance with the principle of storage limitation (article 5.1.e of the GDPR):
• Contact messages: 2 years after the last exchange, then 1 year of intermediate archiving
• Mentora user accounts: as long as the account is active; deleted on request within 30 days, or automatically after 24 months of inactivity
• Newsletter sign-ups: until you unsubscribe (link in every email)
• Technical logs (security, fraud prevention): 12 months maximum
• Encrypted backups: kept for 90 days then destroyed

At the end of these periods, data is anonymized (aggregated statistics) or permanently deleted.

rights.title: Your GDPR rights
rights.body:
You have the following rights at all times, exercisable directly from your account or by email:

• Right of access (article 15): summary of the data we hold, accessible from Community → Settings.
• Right to rectification (article 16): direct edits from your profile.
• Right to erasure / "right to be forgotten" (article 17): "Delete my account" button in Settings → 30-day grace period then irreversible purge.
• Right to restrict processing (article 18): on request to the DPO (Data Protection Officer).
• Right to portability (article 20): JSON export downloadable from Settings, in machine-readable format.
• Right to object (article 21): 1-click marketing opt-out in every email or via Settings.
• Right to withdraw your consent at any time.
• Right to define post-mortem directives on your data (French Data Protection Act).

To exercise a right not covered by the built-in tools, write to contact@digizelle.fr specifying the nature of your request. Reply within 30 days (90 days in case of complexity, with notification). In case of disagreement, you can lodge a complaint with the CNIL — 3 place de Fontenoy, 75007 Paris — www.cnil.fr.

subprocessors.title: Subprocessors & technical partners
subprocessors.body:
To operate, the site relies on subprocessors within the meaning of article 28 of the GDPR. All have been selected for their GDPR compliance and are bound by a Data Processing Agreement (DPA). The list, up to date as of the last revision, is:

— Vercel Inc. (United States, EU infrastructure): Next.js hosting, CDN, serverless functions. Execution region: Europe (CDG / Paris). Data processed: HTTP logs, request content during execution.
— Supabase Inc. (United States, EU infrastructure): PostgreSQL database, authentication, file storage. Region: eu-west-3 (Paris). Data processed: accounts, profiles, community content, mentorship sessions.
— Resend Inc. (United States, EU routing available): sending of transactional emails (verification, reset, notifications) and newsletter campaigns. Data processed: email, first name, deliverability status.
— Functional Software Inc. (Sentry): observability and error reporting on the server and browser side. Region: sentry.de.io (EU). Recorded sessions (replay) mask all text (`maskAllText`) and block media (`blockAllMedia`); request bodies are filtered before sending.
— Auth.js v5 (open-source, run on Digizelle infrastructure): session and authentication management.
— Google LLC, GitHub Inc., Discord Inc.: optional OAuth providers for social sign-in. If you don't use these options, no data is transmitted to them.

None of these subprocessors are authorized to use your data on their own account.

transfers.title: Transfers outside the European Union

minors.title: Minors & youth protection
minors.body:
Digizelle is primarily aimed at a young audience. French law sets the age of digital consent at 15 (article 8 of the GDPR, transposed into article 45 of the amended French Data Protection Act).

• The sign-up form includes a mandatory honor declaration of being at least 15 years old (unchecked checkbox). Without this declaration, sign-up is refused.
• For people aged 15 to 17, sign-up is allowed but no advertising profiling is applied and moderation is reinforced.
• For anyone under 15, sign-up is forbidden. If discovered later (report, profile contradiction), the account is suspended within 24 hours then deleted after verification.
• Parents or guardians may at any time request access, rectification or deletion of their child's data at contact@digizelle.fr by providing proof of their status.
• A Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) is being prepared for the parts that involve minors (public profiles, UGC community).

security.title: Security measures
security.body:
Digizelle implements appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your data against loss, alteration or unauthorized access:
• TLS 1.3 encryption on all communications (HTTPS enforced via HSTS)
• Passwords stored hashed with bcrypt (cost ≥ 12), never in plain text
• Reinforced authentication for administrator accounts
• Supabase Row-Level Security (RLS) policies to compartmentalize data
• Daily encrypted backups, with tested restorability
• Logging of sensitive accesses, alerts in case of anomaly
• Regular security audits and rapid patch application
• Awareness training for volunteers with data access (internal charter)

In the event of a data breach likely to result in a risk to your rights, we will notify the CNIL within 72 hours (article 33 GDPR) and inform you individually if necessary.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.body: The site uses a minimal number of cookies, mainly technical (session, theme preference). No advertising tracking or third-party profiling cookies are placed without your explicit consent, in accordance with article 82 of the French Data Protection Act and CNIL recommendations. See our dedicated Cookies page for the detailed list and management options.

contact.title: Data Protection Officer (DPO)
contact.body:
Digizelle has appointed a dedicated GDPR contact point (internal DPO), independent of the operational functions concerned by the processing:

• GDPR / DPO email: contact@digizelle.fr (the association's single contact address)
• Mail: Digizelle — Data Protection Officer — EPITECH Le Kremlin-Bicêtre Campus, 14-16 rue Voltaire, 94270 Le Kremlin-Bicêtre

DPO duties (article 39 GDPR): inform and advise the data controller, monitor compliance, single point of contact for data subjects and the CNIL, follow-up on incidents and any notifications (articles 33-34).

Response time for requests: 30 days (extendable to 90 days in case of complexity, with reasoned notification). In case of disagreement, you can lodge a complaint with the CNIL — 3 place de Fontenoy, 75007 Paris — www.cnil.fr.

programs.metaDescription: Immersive Workshop, Masterclass, Hackathon, Mentora — four inclusive Digizelle formats.
programs.title: Four formats.
programs.intro: Pick the program that matches your pace, level and interests. All are accessible, all are free or fairly priced.

atelier.title: Immersive Workshop
atelier.desc: Hands-on sessions to explore a digital skill as a group.

details.intro: Three days to discover a digital skill with no prerequisites: you set up, you practice, you ship a mini-project. Designed for school holidays and young people who want a taste of the role before committing further.
details.audience: Ages 15-25, beginners or intermediate. No technical prerequisites — only the desire to experiment.
details.format: 12 participants maximum, 2 professional facilitators throughout, hosted at Epitech or partner spaces. Equipment provided.
details.outcomes: Mini-project delivered, Digizelle certificate, access to the online community and the Programs newsletter to discover upcoming masterclasses and hackathons.

masterclass.title: Masterclass
masterclass.desc: Learning cycle with experts to deepen your knowledge in a technical field.

details.intro: Eight weeks, eight 3-hour sessions led by experts from Microsoft, AWS, Allianz and Epitech. You walk away with a delivered project, validated by a jury and recognized by partner recruiters.
details.audience: Intermediate to advanced level (students with a 2-year degree minimum or experienced self-taught learners). Selection based on motivation letter.
details.format: Weekly in-person sessions (Epitech Le Kremlin-Bicêtre) or hybrid. Followed by an industry mentor throughout the program.
details.outcomes: Digizelle certification, capstone project for your portfolio, possible recommendation to partner companies (Microsoft, AWS, Allianz).

hackathon.title: Hackathon
hackathon.desc: 48 hours to build as a team on a digital challenge with mentors.

details.intro: A weekend to go from an idea to a prototype defended in front of a jury of industry pros. Themes focused on tech & impact (inclusion, climate, responsible AI). Meals, venue and mentors included.
details.audience: All levels from age 16. Sign up as a team (4-5 people) or solo — we'll match you on the day.
details.format: 48 hours non-stop with two on-site nights possible, 8-12 mentors rotating throughout, final 5-minute demo before a jury of 5 pros.
details.outcomes: Winner's prize (equipment + sponsorship donated to an inclusion project), audience favorite prize, introductions to recruiters and project publication on the Digizelle platform.

mentora.title: Mentora
mentora.desc: 1-to-1 mentorship program for sustained, longer-term support.

details.intro: Six months of support with a dedicated mentor. Personal goals, regular video sessions, custom resources and tracking on the Mentora platform. Ideal for those who need clear direction on their career path or entry into the working world.
details.audience: Ages 16-30, students or young professionals looking for direction, a career change or career structuring in digital.
details.format: Algorithmic matching based on your interests and needs, 1 video call per month minimum + unlimited messaging, access to the Mentora platform with goals, sessions and journal.
details.outcomes: Clarified career plan, broader professional network, end-of-program certificate, opportunity to become a mentor yourself afterwards.

detailsModal.loginNote: You'll be redirected to the sign-in or sign-up page.

projects.metaDescription: Digizelle platform, Mentora program, community space and Impact events, hackathons & on-the-ground workshops — all of the association's achievements.
projects.title: What we've
projects.intro: The web platform, the mentorship program, the community and on-the-ground events — every piece is designed with the Digizelle community for the digital inclusion of young people.

0.title: Digizelle Platform
0.desc: The website and the app together: showcase, Mentora program and community space.

1.title: Mentora
1.desc: Mentor/mentee program with algorithmic matching, scheduled sessions, goals and messaging. Designed for young people far from digital opportunities.

2.title: Digizelle Community
2.desc: Online community space: feed, themed channels, badges, challenges. More than 200 active members daily.

3.title: Digizelle Impact #1
3.desc: Our flagship event on March 13, 2026 at Epitech Paris. Over 60 young people, panels with Microsoft · AWS · Allianz and a leadership keynote.

4.title: Women in GenAI Hackathon
4.desc: EY × Microsoft 2025 finalist. Digizelle team (Bleuenn, Inès, Rajeeva, Marine) on a GenAI project for inclusion.

5.title: On-the-ground Workshop Series
5.desc: In-person workshops at VivaTech, BECOMTECH × Epitech, HEC Paris. Topics: AI as my future career, imposter syndrome, leadership.

projects.eventsCtaBody: Panels, hackathons, workshops — all our editions in one place.

team.metaDescription: The Digizelle team: co-founders, leads, volunteers and mentors committed to digital inclusion.
team.title: The faces
team.intro: Digizelle brings together more than 20 members with diverse backgrounds — design, development, communication, marketing, events — united around a shared mission: making digital accessible to everyone.

5.name: Rajeeva Ravichandiran

6.name: Marine Fruitier

join.title: Want to
join.body: We're always looking for mentors, speakers, event volunteers and corporate partners. One hour a month or more — every contribution counts.
